summary: Changed tools/revert_asm.py so unlinking a reverted C source from obj_files.mk is opt-in instead of the default. The old --keep/-k flag suppressed unlinking; this PR inverted that behavior to a clearer --unlink/-u flag that only edits obj_files.mk when explicitly requested.
what_changed: In tools/revert_asm.py, the obj_files.mk edit condition changed from `if not args.keep:` to `if args.unlink:`.
The command-line flag changed from `--keep`/`-k` with help text `keep the entry in obj_files.mk` to `--unlink`/`-u` with help text `unlink the C file in obj_files.mk`.
The script still builds `all_source`, copies the object output to the ASM path, and only then optionally modifies obj_files.mk; the PR only inverted the default unlinking behavior.
smart_moves: Made the destructive/project-structure-changing part of `revert_asm` opt-in. By default, reverting ASM no longer removes the C file entry from obj_files.mk.
Replaced a negated option (`--keep`) with a positive action option (`--unlink`), making call sites and user intent easier to read.
Kept the change minimal: only the boolean condition and CLI argument definition changed, reducing risk in a workflow tool.
decomp_lessons: For decomp tooling that mutates build metadata such as obj_files.mk, prefer explicit opt-in flags for unlinking/removal actions.
Avoid CLI flags whose default behavior is surprising or encoded as a negative condition like `if not args.keep`; use positive verbs like `--unlink` when the action is optional.
Small tooling behavior changes can matter for matching workflow safety because accidental unlinking can disrupt active C decompilation work.
naming_conventions: Use action-oriented CLI flag names for optional mutations: `--unlink` / `-u` communicates that obj_files.mk will be changed.
The help text should describe the concrete target of the action: `unlink the C file in obj_files.mk`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `revert_asm.py` supports reverting a C source back to an ASM object path by running `ninja all_source` and copying the built object output to the ASM path.
When reverting code for matching or fallback purposes, separating ASM restoration from obj_files.mk unlinking lets contributors preserve build metadata unless they deliberately request unlinking.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or issues mentioning `revert_asm --unlink` to see whether the inverted default resolved accidental obj_files.mk edits.
Search for documentation or scripts that still reference `revert_asm --keep` or `-k` after this PR.
Check whether other decomp tooling has negated flags for destructive behavior that should be converted to explicit opt-in actions.
